data.title: U.S. selected services revenue gains 2.2% in Q3: Quarterly Services Survey
data.description:
Q3 2024 Quarterly Services Report: U.S. selected services total revenue rose 2.2% Q/Q $5,735.4B, accelerating from the 1.6% revised increase in Q2, the U.S. Census Bureau said on Thursday. The latest figure was also up 8.4% from a year earlier.

Utilities revenue for Q3 decreased 1.4% Q/Q and advanced 3.2% Y/Y to $186.0B.

Transportation and warehousing revenue of $353.5B rose 1.2% from the previous three-month period and 4.8% from last year's Q3.

Information sector revenue was $613.5B, up 2.2% from Q2 and 7.7% Y/Y.

Finance and insurance revenue of $1,757.0B was 2.1% higher from Q2 and 9.5% higher from Q3 2023.

Real estate and rental leasing revenue increased 1.4% Q/Q and 6.6% Y/Y to $262.5B.

Health care and social assistance revenue stood at $1,020.7B, up 3.7% sequentially and 12.1% from a year before, the Census Bureau said.

data.title: Visa Direct to speed funds availability for U.S. cardholders
data.description:
Visa's (NYSE:V) direct payment service will make funds available to U.S. cardholders' bank accounts within a minute or less starting in April 2025, the company said Thursday.

Consumers, businesses, and governments can use Visa Direct to deposit funds to bank accounts linked to eligible debit cards in real time.

The faster funds availability can enhance organizations' operational efficiency, deliver better customer experiences, and foster trust among their clients, Visa (NYSE:V) said.

Visa (V) stock edged up 0.1% in Thursday premarket trading.

data.title: Gold set for modest growth in 2025, WGC says, China will be key
data.description:
With gold prices (XAUUSD:CUR) set for their best annual performance in more than a decade, the Federal Reserve and the direction of the U.S. dollar will continue to be important drivers for the asset in 2025, the World Gold Council said.

While lower yields and a weakening U.S. dollar in Q3 fueled Western investment flows, it is gold’s role as a hedge amidst rising market volatility and geopolitical risk that most likely explains its remarkable performance, WGC said, as it expects a positive, but "much more modest growth for gold in 2025."

"(Donald) Trump’s second term may provide a boost to the local economy but could equally elicit a fair degree of nervousness for investors around the world," the Gold Outlook 2025 report released on December 12 said.

Bullion has increased by more than 28% since the beginning of the year, and reached 40 record highs, with total gold demand in the third quarter surpassing $100 billion for the first time.

Thrill-seeking investors may benefit from an early wave of risk-on flows, but potential trade wars and inflationary forces may spill over into an expected subpar economic growth, the report suggests.

An upside could come from stronger-than-expected central bank demand, or from a rapid deterioration of financial conditions leading to flight-to-quality flows, the report said.

Conversely, a reversal in monetary policy, leading to higher interest rates, is seen bringing challenges.

Overall, gold is likely to remain range bound if existing market expectations are correct.

However, a combination of higher rates and lower economic growth could negatively affect investors and consumers. This could be particularly evident in Asia, WGC concluded, adding that China’s contribution to the gold market will be key.

data.title: Non-alcoholic beverages set to benefit in 2025 from impulse buying, convenience store traffic - analyst
data.description:
Consumer value consciousness remains elevated exiting 2024, and there are still pockets of defensiveness within the consumer products category. But increased impulse buying, and more persuasive promotions leads Deutsche Bank to take a bullish posture on certain names within the category, pivoting towards non-alcoholic beverages with an upgrade to Coca-Cola (NYSE:KO), PepsiCo (PEP), and Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP).

“If there is one area where we are more optimistic on the potential for accelerating trends heading into 2025 it resides in restaurant/convenience store traffic and more impulse purchasing—dynamics that should work to the advantage of companies operating in the beverage (and snacks) industry with elevated exposure to such channels,” Deutsche analysts Steve Powers and Christopher Barnes write in Thursday’s research note on the CPG sector.

The team sees most investors assuming an offensive stance into 2025 to the detriment of defensive sectors, while fundamentals favor the U.S. given the likelihood for “slower-than-ideal” recoveries in China and the Middle East, and risks of deceleration in Europe and Latin America.

Against that backdrop, Deutsche Bank upgrades Coca-Cola (KO) to Buy with a $70 price target as the company’s underlying business momentum remains exceptionally strong with positive volume growth in each of the last three years despite double-digit average pricing. While there remain headwinds from foreign-exchange fluctuations and health-conscious consumers rejecting sugary beverages, Coca-Cola’s (KO) focus on affordability and the company’s ability to navigate hyperinflation and volatile FX conditions should serve the company well.

PepsiCo (PEP) was also upgraded to Buy, but in this case, there are more challenging headwinds for the company to overcome. North American momentum has been “lackluster,” there are lasting impacts from the Quaker Foods production disruption, and the international business slowed versus expectations. But Deutsche Bank sees ample productivity and cost flexibility cushioning forward earnings while still allowing investments to drive sequential improvement/recovery in the Frito-Lay and PepsiCo North America segment. Deutsche Bank sets a $184 price target for PEP, a 17% upside to Wednesday’s close.

For Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP), there remains longer-term questions surrounding the ability to leverage and drive sustained economic profit from the refreshment beverages network, as well as the runway for growth in Keurig's coffee ecosystem. That said, KDP is managing against these questions correctly, putting the company in the best possible position against Deutsche Bank’s would-be objections such as investing assertively in its core refreshment beverage brands while at the same time seeking out promising partnerships, and maximizing the current K-cup uptake by focusing on affordability and innovations around cold coffee. Deutsche Bank upgrades KDP to Buy from Hold with a $39 price target, 17% upside to the prior close.

With these factors along with “historically rational competition over the past decade and relative pricing power, we see preconditions for outperformance – especially when considering starting point valuations that remain depressed relative to history and peer subsectors,” Powers and Barnes conclude.

Separately, the team initiated coverage of Celsius Holdings (CELH) with a Hold rating as the company’s potential for reaccelerated shipments and consumption is offset by an assumed heightened level of promotional activity for the brand and questions around the durability of long-term double-digit growth without a core SKU or flavor exceeding 10% of sales.

data.title: Calidi Biotherapeutics prices $7.5M secondary offering
data.description:
• Calidi Biotherapeutics (NYSE:CLDI) announced secondary offering of ~6.94M shares

  • The offering comprises of 2.05M shares issuable on the exercise of Series E common warrants, exercisable at an exercise price of $1.13 per share, 2.05M shares of Common Stock issuable on the exercise of Series F common warrants, exercisable at an exercise price of $1.13 per share, and 221,893 shares of Common Stock issued to Ladenburg

  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of securities.
  • The closing of the offering took place on November 15, 2024.
  • The gross proceeds from the offering were approximately $7.5M.

data.title: Lovesac sinks after issuing soft guidance due to category headwinds
data.description:
The Lovesac Company (NASDAQ:LOVE) fell sharply in early trading on Thursday after reporting a 2.7% year-over-year drop in revenue during Q3 to miss the consensus estimate by $5.4 million. The decline during the quarter was driven by a decrease of 8.3% in omnichannel comparable sales, partially offset by the addition of 28 new showrooms.

Gross margin increased 110 basis points to 58.5% of sales in the quarter, primarily driven by decreases of 120 basis points in inbound transportation costs and 40 basis points in outbound transportation and warehousing costs, partially offset by a decrease of 50 basis points in product margin driven by higher promotional discounting. EPS was reported at -$0.32 vs. -$0.35 consensus and -$0.15 a year ago.

CEO Shawn Nelson said despite near-term headwinds for the category clearly that persisted through the pre-election period, the company gained market share and strengthened its competitive position through its focus on product innovation and operational excellence. Nelson said the soft launch of its reclining seat in the quarter is just one of many exciting examples on the way.

Looking ahead, Lovesac (LOVE) sees Q4 revenue of $221 million to $241 million vs. $269 million and EPS of $1.67 to $2.14 vs. $2.53 consensus.

Shares of Lovesac Company (LOVE) nosedived 22.1% in the first 15 minutes of trading on Thursday. The company has an investor event schedule for next week.

data.title: Terreno Realty acquires property in Brooklyn, New York, for ~$156.3M
data.description:
• Industrial REIT Terreno Realty (NYSE:TRNO) acquired a property in Red Hook, Brooklyn, New York, on December 11 for ~$156.3M.
  • The purchase price includes the assumption of ~$72.9M 3.85% interest-only loan, maturing in March 2028.
  • The property consists of one 36-foot clear height industrial distribution building containing ~312K leasable sq. ft. on 16 acres.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Qualcomm appoints Baaziz Achour to the role of CTO
data.description:
In a key leadership transition, Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) on Thursday appointed Baaziz Achour to the role of Chief Technology Officer, effective February 3, 2025.

Achour, who currently serves as deputy Chief Technology Officer, will succeed James Thompson, the company said. Thompson is set to retire as CTO after 33 years at the company.

Achour has first joined Qualcomm as a Systems Engineer in 1993 and has held several leadership roles within the engineering organization over the course of his tenure. According to the company, he played an important role in the accelerated launch of 5G and is also expected to lead the company's evolution to 6G.

“During his time as CTO, Jim not only advanced Qualcomm’s wireless technology leadership but also expanded our capabilities, including in advanced processors and AI,” said Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon.

Shares of the California-based chipmaker were down over 1% in morning trading on Thursday. Overall, the stock has gained 9% so far this year, compared to the nearly 28% rise in the broader S&P500 Index.

data.title: Wall Street wavers after producer inflation data comes in hot
data.description:
U.S. stocks were largely lower on Thursday after the opening bell, following some key producer inflation data that came in hotter than expected.

Minutes after market open, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.20%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.36%. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.02%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 2 basis points to 4.29%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was up 1 basis point to 4.17%.

Before the start of regular trading, the November Producer Price Index (PPI) came in at +0.4% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus, and +3.0% Y/Y vs. +2.6%. Core PPI came in at +0.2% M/M vs. +0.2% consensus, and +3.4% Y/Y vs. +3.2% consensus.

Separately, initial jobless claims unexpectedly jumped in the past week.

U.S. stocks finished mostly higher in Wednesday's history-making session, as an in-line consumer inflation report bolstered expectations of another quarter-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve next week. Nasdaq had ended above 20K, with the Big Tech boosting the index.

"Markets put in a decent performance yesterday, as the U.S. CPI report came in broadly as expected, which was seen as giving the all-clear for the Federal Reserve to cut rates next week," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"When it comes to the Fed, the CPI print left little doubt among investors that another rate cut will happen next week," Allen added.

Adobe (ADBE) shares fell 10.2% after the Photoshop creator issued guidance for fiscal 2025 that fell short of Wall Street's expectations.
